steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage uses 'However' to signal a contrast: the three-host CLP's abundance dropped with rising temperature while the directly transmitted parasite's abundance stayed stable. The correct choice must explain that contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'CLPs were less dependent on temperature-affected hosts': Contradicts the data — CLPs' abundance did drop, indicating they were more dependent. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the advantages of three-host transmission did not fully offset the negative temperature effects': Matches the observed drop in CLP abundance despite any benefits of using multiple hosts. ✓
  • Choice C — 'directly transmitted parasites were more shielded from temperature': The passage shows stable abundance for directly transmitted parasites, but this choice misattributes the mechanism in a way the data doesn't support. ✗
  • Choice D — 'more hosts provide better protection from temperature effects': Conflicts with the data, which show the opposite — more hosts correlated with a drop, not protection. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
